names.scientific_name: Chlorophyll a and chlorophyll b (mixture of chlorophyll compounds)

natural_sources: all green leafy vegetables and plants
natural_occurrence.description: The actual pigment that makes plants green and enables photosynthesis. Chlorophyll is present in chloroplasts of all green plants. Structurally similar to hemoglobin (oxygen-carrying pigment in blood) - chlorophyll has magnesium where hemoglobin has iron. Produces one-third to one-half of Earth's atmospheric oxygen through photosynthesis.

manufacturing.description: Extracted from chloroplasts of green plant materials (spinach, alfalfa, nettles) using food-grade solvents such as ethanol or acetone. The chlorophyll retains its natural magnesium ion in the porphyrin ring structure. The extracted chlorophyll is purified and standardized.

other_uses.cosmetics: Natural green colorant in cosmetic products

eu.max_level_mg_kg: quantum satis in most food categories
eu.conditions: Generally permitted with quantum satis; no specific restrictions

usa.notes: Despite being completely natural, USA does not permit chlorophyll as a colour additive in food, drugs, or cosmetics

japan.notes: Approved as existing food additive (natural origin)

safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: unable to establish (2015 - insufficient toxicological data)

regulatory_history.event: JECFA established 'not limited' ADI at 13th meeting (generally recognized as safe at reported use levels)
EFSA re-evaluation found impossible to establish specific ADI due to limited toxicological data, but concluded 'not of safety concern at reported use levels'

commonly_found_in: Spinach pasta and green pasta varieties
Natural health supplements (liquid chlorophyll, chlorophyll pills)

adi_context.source_note: JECFA established 'not limited' ADI in 1969. EFSA was unable to establish a numerical ADI in 2015 due to insufficient toxicological data, but concluded that chlorophyll is 'not of safety concern at reported use levels' based on its natural occurrence and widespread consumption in vegetables. No daily limit established.
